intro: Solms-laubachia mieheorum, also known as the Miège's Alpine Cress, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Solms-laubachia mieheorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Solms-laubachia mieheorum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
